Assessed: $334,000 — Listed Below Assessed
Wayne and Michelle — This is a rare fully-serviced building lot in one of Estes Park’s established luxury subdivisions. Unlike most mountain lots I see in this market, Lakota Ct has municipal water and sewer tap availability — which eliminates two of the biggest cost and complexity unknowns in a mountain custom build. The 2026 assessed value is $334,000; it’s listed at $290,000. That gap is worth noting. Views of Twin Sisters, Prospect Mountain, and the Mummy Range with a gentle slope that creates strong walkout potential. Here’s everything you need to evaluate it. — Zachary

🌟 Why the Below-Assessed Price Matters: Larimer County assessed this lot at $334,000 for 2026 tax purposes. The ask of $290,000 represents a 13% discount to assessed value — unusual for an Estes Park lot with utilities in place. This doesn’t mean the lot is “worth” $334K automatically, but it signals the county’s independent valuation is well above asking and suggests motivated seller pricing. The Chavez Family Trust purchased in 2016 for $139,000 — still realizing a significant gain at $290K after a decade of holding.
✅ What Works for You Here: Municipal water and sewer availability is the headline advantage. Custom mountain builds in the Estes corridor routinely face $30,000–$80,000+ in well and septic costs alone. This lot has water and sewer tap availability at the cul-de-sac — eliminating those unknowns entirely. Electric, natural gas, and data lines are underground at the curb. The infrastructure is done. You’re buying a blank canvas with services already at the door.
⚠️ Things to Weigh: Highway 7 (S. Saint Vrain Ave) runs along the southern boundary — this is a two-lane state highway with moderate traffic. Depending on home orientation and setback, this may or may not be a daily factor. A site visit at different times of day will tell you more than any report can. Noxious weeds have been identified and treated with herbicide (disclosed in SPD). Standard for this terrain; ongoing maintenance is typical.
⚡
Utility Infrastructure
What’s in place, what’s needed, and what it means for your build budget
📌 Key Finding: This lot has one of the strongest utility profiles available in the Estes Park area for a raw building parcel. All major services are underground at the cul-de-sac curb — ready for tap connections. This significantly simplifies permitting and reduces construction timeline uncertainty compared to lots requiring well drilling or septic design/installation.
Utility Status Provider / Details Budget Impact
Water ✅ Tap Available Town of Estes Park — 170 MacGregor Ave · 970-577-4800 · estespark.colorado.gov/utilities/water — Municipal system confirmed per Source of Water Addendum (SWA) signed June 1, 2026 Eliminates well cost
Well would add $25K–$70K+
Sewer ✅ Tap Available Sewer Tap Available per IRES MLS data and lot improvements disclosure. Eliminates septic system design, installation, and Larimer County Engineering review requirements. Eliminates septic cost
Septic would add $18K–$45K+
Electric ✅ Underground at Curb Underground electric at property per IRES and SPD disclosure. Estes Park Utilities or PVREA depending on location. Verify service territory with Town of Estes Park. Minimal extension cost
Tap fee + lateral only
Natural Gas ✅ At Property Natural Gas at property per MLS disclosure. Xcel Energy serves Estes Park area. Gas service simplifies mechanical design for heating and range — no propane tank required. No propane tank needed
Saves $3K–$8K in tank/install
Telephone / Internet ✅ Underground at North End Underground transmission lines for telephone and internet confirmed at north end of property per SPD Section E.4. Verify fiber vs. coax availability with CenturyLink/Lumen or Xfinity for Estes Park address. Verify fiber availability
Typical extension cost minimal
Satellite / High Speed ✅ Available Satellite and high-speed internet available per MLS. Starlink is viable as backup or primary in this location. Recommend confirming wired speeds before finalizing home office plans. Backup option confirmed
Starlink ~$120/mo if needed
Road ✅ Paved Cul-de-Sac Street paved, fire hydrant within 500 feet per IRES. Private road up to county standards. Paved access reduces driveway cost and avoids maintenance assessments common on gravel roads. Driveway extension only
No road improvement needed
📋 Tap Fees — Important Next Step: Water and sewer tap availability is confirmed, but actual tap fees are paid at time of building permit and can be substantial in Estes Park. Contact the Town of Estes Park Utilities Department (970-577-4800) to obtain current water tap fee and sewer tap fee schedules before finalizing your budget. These fees are in addition to the lot purchase price and construction costs.
🏗
What Can Be Built Here
Build scenario modeling for a custom mountain home on Lot 11, Kiowa Ridge — Larimer County, Estes Valley Development Code
📌 Regulatory Framework: This lot is within the Town of Estes Park and is subject to the Estes Valley Development Code (EVDC). MLS designates zoning as “R” however the zoning map shows the lot within the Estes Park town boundary in what appears to be the “02” zone, surrounded by EV RE (Estes Valley Rural Estate). Confirm the exact zoning designation and allowable use directly with Estes Park Community Development (970-577-3720) before finalizing any build plans. One unit is allowed per MLS disclosure. Unlike Boulder County, Estes Park / Larimer County does not require net-zero energy compliance for new residential construction. BuildSmart or HERS certification requirements do not apply here. Applicable codes are IBC/IRC as adopted by Larimer County and the Town of Estes Park, including wildfire-resilient construction standards under Larimer County Wildfire Partners program. A pre-application meeting with Estes Park Community Development (970-577-3720) is advisable before finalizing plans.
Option A
Mountain Cottage
1,800 – 2,200 sq ft · 3 bed / 2 bath
$650K – $850K
Total project est. incl. lot — land at $290K + construction
  • Single-level or 1.5-story footprint using slope for walkout
  • Open kitchen with island — your stated priority
  • Primary suite with 5-piece bath and walk-in closet
  • Covered deck oriented toward Twin Sisters / Mummy Range
  • Efficient footprint reduces per-sq-ft site work costs
  • Construction: est. $200–$260/sq ft for quality mountain build
Best for: maximizing lot and view while keeping total project under $900K. Most likely to replicate the feel of your Colorado Springs home at a comparable or lower total cost.
Option B — Best Fit
Custom Mountain Home
2,400 – 3,200 sq ft · 3–4 bed / 3 bath
$900K – $1.15M
Total project est. incl. lot — land at $290K + construction
  • Two-story or walkout ranch leveraging the gentle slope
  • Generous open-concept great room / kitchen with island
  • Primary suite on main level — mountain view orientation
  • Lower level flex space: guest suite, office, or rec room
  • Layered outdoor living: main deck + lower patio
  • Quality kitchen hardware and surfaces — no compromise
  • Construction: est. $250–$290/sq ft for custom mountain finish
Best for: a home that truly matches what you love in Colorado Springs. This range delivers the open kitchen, the primary suite, the views, and the entertaining space — all within your stated budget with appropriate contingency.
Option C
Luxury Mountain Estate
3,500 – 4,500 sq ft · 4–5 bed / 4+ bath
$1.2M – $1.6M+
Total project est. incl. lot — land at $290K + construction
  • Full custom design with architect — view-maximizing orientation
  • Chef’s kitchen: custom cabinetry, premium appliances, island seating
  • Primary suite with spa bath, steam, heated floors
  • Dedicated office, media room, or studio space
  • Three-car garage possible on this footprint
  • Construction: est. $275–$320/sq ft for high-end custom finish
Best for: buyers who want no compromises on finishes and space. The lot can support this footprint. Budget contingency at this tier should be 15–20% given current Estes Park subcontractor market.

ItemEst. RangeNotes
Water tap feeTBD — verifyTown of Estes Park · 970-577-4800
Sewer tap feeTBD — verifyPaid at building permit issuance
Electric service lateral$3,000 – $8,000From curb to house panel
Gas service lateral$2,000 – $5,000From main to meter
Driveway$8,000 – $20,000Varies by length & surface
Site grading / prep$15,000 – $40,000Slope is gentle — favorable

Landscaping / revegetation$12,000 – $35,000HOA may require plan approval

🕑 Estimated Build Timeline

Month 1–3
Lot purchase closes → Architect selection → Site survey and geotechnical report → Preliminary design
Month 4–7
Final design → HOA architectural review (Kiowa Ridge) → Building permit submittal → Contractor bidding
Month 8–10
Permit issuance → Site work and foundation → Framing → Rough mechanicals
Month 11–18
Insulation → Drywall → Finish work → Kitchen & baths → Final inspections → Certificate of Occupancy
Total est. timeline: 16–22 months from lot purchase to move-in. Estes Park has a limited contractor pool — builder selection early is critical. Budget 10–15% contingency on all construction estimates.

🔎 Buyer Advisory — Recommended Next Steps Before Offer: (1) Contact Kiowa Ridge HOA to obtain CC&Rs, architectural guidelines, and any restrictions on home size, exterior materials, or landscaping requirements. (2) Request current water tap fee and sewer tap fee schedule from Town of Estes Park Utilities. (3) Verify internet service speeds at this address — contact CenturyLink/Lumen and Xfinity. (4) Commission a boundary survey and topographic survey to confirm slope, setbacks, and home positioning for view optimization. (5) Consult a Larimer County-licensed architect or builder for a pre-application meeting with Estes Park Community Development before finalizing any build plans.

Build Cost Estimates

Construction cost estimates, infrastructure cost ranges, and project timeline estimates presented herein are informational approximations based on general Northern Colorado / Estes Park area market conditions as of 2026 and are not bids, contractor quotes, or guaranteed figures. Actual costs will vary based on design, materials, site conditions, contractor availability, permit fees, and market conditions at time of construction. Buyers are strongly advised to obtain independent estimates from licensed Colorado contractors and architects before making any financial commitments. Tap fees for water and sewer must be verified directly with the Town of Estes Park Utilities Department prior to purchase.

Reliability & Verification

Information presented is deemed reliable but is not guaranteed and is subject to verification, errors, omissions, prior sale, or withdrawal. Square footage, acreage, zoning classifications, setback requirements, view representations, HOA fees, and utility availability are approximate and must be independently verified prior to any purchase decision.

Not Legal, Financial, or Professional Advice

This report does not constitute legal, tax, financial, appraisal, surveying, engineering, environmental, or investment advice. Purchasers are encouraged to conduct independent investigation including structural inspection, environmental assessment, title examination, survey, wildfire hazard assessment, and consultation with appropriate licensed professionals before entering any purchase contract.

Wildfire & Natural Hazard Notice

Properties in the Estes Park area and surrounding Larimer County mountain communities may be located in or adjacent to Wildland-Urban Interface (WUI) zones. Buyers should independently verify wildfire hazard designations, defensible space requirements, and homeowner’s insurance availability and cost prior to purchase. Flood plain data does not reflect Letters of Map Amendment or other official map changes.
